Title: Functional genomics dissection of the nodulation autoregulation pathway (AON) in soybean (Glycine max)

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nitrogen / fixed-nitrogen acquisition via symbiotic nodulation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nodulation by moving from shoot to root and repressing TML; suppression of miR2111 by AON reduces nodulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Petiole feeding of synthetic mature miR2111 RNA; overexpression; target mimicry; nodulation phenotyping
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR2111 is a shoot-derived mobile signal in legumes including soybean. 2. Shoot perception of rhizobia-induced CLE peptides via GmNARK suppresses miR2111 expression. 3. Reduced miR2111 allows TML accumulation in roots and inhibits nodule organogenesis; increasing miR2111 increases nodule number.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively synthesized in shoot; suppressed after AON activation; upregulated under low nitrate conditions in legumes
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated miR2111 availability promotes nodulation and alters root architecture, enhancing lateral root emergence while decreasing lateral root length and average root diameter.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to AON shoot-to-root signaling, the review states miR2111 is not necessarily the historically proposed SDI itself.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A candidate module for engineering nodulation and root architecture by tuning shoot-to-root signaling, but excessive nodulation and root architectural changes should be avoided.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nitrogen / fixed-nitrogen acquisition via symbiotic nodulation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of nodulation in roots; accumulation inhibits nodule organogenesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Target mimicry and miR2111 functional studies referenced in soybean and legumes; nodulation phenotyping
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TML is a central root-localized negative regulator in the AON pathway. 2. Shoot-derived miR2111 suppresses TML transcripts, permitting nodulation. 3. Upon GmNARK activation, miR2111 decreases and TML accumulates, attenuating nodulation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: TML mutants show weaker phenotypes than GmNARK mutants, implying additional pathways and possible limited standalone utility.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recognized as central regulator of AON within roots, but weaker mutant phenotype than GmNARK suggests pathway redundancy or parallel mechanisms.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for moderate manipulation of nodulation, potentially safer than disrupting GmNARK, though incomplete effect suggests stacking with other regulators may be required.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nitrogen / fixed-nitrogen acquisition via symbiotic nodulation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for systemic inhibition of target process
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Systemically suppresses nodulation through GmNARK after rhizobial induction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in soybean; systemic nodulation phenotyping; genetic dependency on GmNARK inferred from cited studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmRIC1 is a rhizobia-induced CLE peptide gene in soybean. 2. It acts upstream of GmNARK in the AON pathway. 3. It is proposed to function as a root-to-shoot signal that systemically inhibits nodulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root nodules / root system as the regulated organ
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely xylem-mobile and functionally analogous to tri-arabinosylated CLE peptides in other legumes, though direct transport evidence in soybean is inferred rather than shown here.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to reduce excessive nodulation under conditions favoring external nitrogen supply; manipulation should consider impacts on symbiotic nitrogen fixation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nitrogen / fixed-nitrogen acquisition via symbiotic nodulation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for systemic inhibition of target process
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Systemically suppresses nodulation through GmNARK after rhizobial induction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in soybean; systemic nodulation phenotyping; GmNARK-dependent functional interpretation from cited studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmRIC2 is a rhizobia-induced CLE peptide gene in soybean. 2. It acts in the root-to-shoot arm of AON. 3. It contributes to systemic inhibition of nodulation via GmNARK.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root nodules / root system as the regulated organ
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review groups GmRIC1 and GmRIC2 together as GmNARK-dependent root-to-shoot signals; direct biochemical binding in soybean is speculative based on cross-legume evidence.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering lever for nodulation homeostasis; must be balanced against the need for adequate nitrogen fixation in low-input systems.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nitrogen / fixed-nitrogen acquisition via symbiotic nodulation
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nitrate-mediated nodulation inhibition via CLE signaling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for local inhibition of target process
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Locally suppresses nodulation in response to high nitrate through GmNARK in the root.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in transgenic roots; local nodulation phenotyping in composite plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmNIC1 is a nitrate-induced CLE peptide gene in soybean. 2. It mediates local nitrate inhibition of nodulation through GmNARK. 3. Its effect is local rather than systemic, distinguishing nitrate inhibition from AON.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root nodules / root system as the regulated organ
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes a related GmNIC1/2 designation in the model figure, but the text explicitly discusses GmNIC1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising target to uncouple nodulation from nitrate sensitivity in breeding, but reduced nitrate responsiveness may compromise adaptive nitrogen-use efficiency.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nitrogen / fixed-nitrogen acquisition via symbiotic nodulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Systemic autoregulation of nodulation; nitrate-related nodulation inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical shoot regulator of AON; essential receptor controlling systemic suppression of nodule formation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: miR2111 synthesis in shoot; downstream root GmTML accumulation; GmRR11d activation/function; perception of GmRIC1/GmRIC2 and GmNIC1 signals
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses miR2111 production after CLE perception, thereby indirectly promoting TML accumulation and inhibiting nodulation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedback regulation in AON root-shoot-root loop
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function causes supernodulation/hypernodulation, partial nitrate tolerance of nodulation, and altered lateral root development.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; reciprocal grafting; positional cloning/map-based cloning; allele sequencing; nodulation phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmNARK encodes the central CLV1-like LRR receptor kinase of soybean AON. 2. It acts in the shoot to perceive root-derived CLE signals and suppress further nodulation systemically. 3. Loss-of-function mutants supernodulate, show localized nitrate tolerance of nodulation, and display pleiotropic root developmental changes.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in shoots in AON; also involved in roots for nitrate-mediated local nodulation inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Putative co-receptors in receptor complex; GmRIC1; GmRIC2; GmNIC1
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes excessive nodulation, slower growth, altered root architecture, and pleiotropic developmental effects; strong disruption may reduce agronomic balance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A duplicated homolog, GmCLV1A, retains architectural roles and does not substitute for AON. Direct downstream transcription factor linking GmNARK to miR2111 remains unknown.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major target for tuning nodulation and yield, but engineering should avoid full loss of function; partial alleles or context-dependent modulation may better balance fixation, growth, and nitrate responsiveness.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nitrogen / fixed-nitrogen acquisition via symbiotic nodulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Systemic inhibition of nodulation and cytokinin response
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Governs a transcriptional program associated with nodulation attenuation in soybean downstream of GmNARK
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmNSP1 transcriptional activity on GmNIN1a; possibly GmNIN expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses GmNSP1-mediated activation of GmNIN1a and thereby inhibits nodulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression / attenuation of transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively mediates cytokinin response in roots and inhibits soybean nodulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional analysis referenced by Chen et al. (2022), including transcriptional assays and nodulation phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmRR11d is a B-type response regulator that mediates systemic inhibition of nodulation in soybean. 2. It suppresses the transcriptional activity of GmNSP1 on GmNIN1a. 3. Its induction and function depend on GmNARK, linking cytokinin response to AON output.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in roots after rhizobial signaling in a GmNARK-dependent manner
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Whether shoot-derived cytokinin directly activates GmRR11d remains unresolved.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to fine-tune nodulation attenuation without fully disrupting AON, especially in cytokinin-responsive contexts.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nitrogen / fixed-nitrogen acquisition via symbiotic nodulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulation of nodulation signaling and CLE gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator that restrains nodulation and AON signal peptide gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly inhibits GmRIC1/2 expression and suppresses GmENOD40; also antagonizes GmNIN activation of GmRIC1/2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses nodulation-related gene expression and contributes to nodulation attenuation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic and transcriptional studies cited in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NNC1 antagonizes nodulation signaling in soybean. 2. It directly inhibits GmRIC1/2 and suppresses GmENOD40 expression. 3. It also interacts with GmNIN to dampen activation of CLE genes involved in AON.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Regulated by miR172c in nodulation signaling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review highlights it as a key summarized regulator of CLE signal production relevant to AON.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be manipulated to adjust nodulation intensity and the strength of root-to-shoot AON signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nitrogen / fixed-nitrogen acquisition via symbiotic nodulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of soybean nodulation targeted by miR156b
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of multiple nodulation regulators
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes symbiotic nodulation by activating key nodulation genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced functional genomics study with target-binding/promoter regulation and nodulation phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmSPL9d is a positive regulator of soybean nodulation. 2. It is targeted by miR156b. 3. It binds promoters of GmNINa, miR172c and GmENOD40-1 to regulate nodulation signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: miR156b; rhizobial regulation of miR156b module
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful to enhance nodulation in low-input systems, but possible interactions with developmental programs should be considered.
